ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic kebab food product class; no specific lamb-and-eggplant kebab entry exists. Closest defensible parent class used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific eggplant-lamb kebab entry. A0FKP (lamb meat dish, grilled) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for eggplant lamb kebab; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one skewer of approximately 120 g, comprising minced lamb and eggplant chunks grilled over charcoal.

nutrient_source.title: Egg Plant Kebab Lamb — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Egg Plant Kebab Lamb (cr_id G00010366). All core macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is adapted from USDA FoodData Central ground lamb (cooked) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for eggplant lamb kebab. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on the dominant macronutrient profile: very low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100g), high fat (26.4 g/100g), and moderate protein (10.7 g/100g). Analogous reference: mixed meat-and-vegetable kebab / grilled lamb dishes with negligible starch content, consistent with low-GI classification per University of Sydney GI Database principles. GL per serving = (35 × 5.24 × 1.2) / 100 = 2.2 g (120 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; sodium at 1080 mg/100 g represents a critical load, and even a single 120 g skewer delivers approximately 1296 mg sodium, approaching the entire recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100g) and low GI support glycaemic stability, but high fat content (26.4 g/100g) may impair insulin sensitivity and slow gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is low in carbohydrate and has a low estimated GI of 35, making it broadly suitable for blood glucose management; however, the high saturated fat content (~11 g/100g) may worsen insulin resistance over time and should be consumed in moderation as part of a balanced dietary pattern.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~11 g/100g), cholesterol (85 mg/100g), and critically elevated sodium (1080 mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its high saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium content; the sodium load alone (1080 mg/100g) substantially increases arterial pressure risk, and the saturated fat burden contributes to atherogenic LDL elevation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lamb is a source of arachidonic acid (pro-inflammatory omega-6); eggplant is a nightshade containing solanine which may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals; sodium at 1080 mg/100g worsens tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; lamb provides pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid, eggplant as a nightshade vegetable may trigger joint flares in RA-sensitive individuals, and the high sodium content further promotes inflammatory tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Eggplant Kebab with Lamb is a Middle Eastern-style grilled skewer dish consisting of minced lamb meat and chunks of eggplant (aubergine) threaded onto a skewer and cooked over charcoal, imparting a distinctive smoky flavour. The lamb is typically seasoned with salt and aromatic spices before grilling, while the eggplant softens and absorbs the meat juices and smoke during cooking. The texture is a contrast of tender, slightly charred eggplant and juicy, well-seasoned minced lamb. This dish is popular in Singapore's Middle Eastern and halal food establishments and is commonly served as a main course at lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 302 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 26.4 g fat, and only 5.24 g carbohydrate, making it a high-fat, low-carbohydrate dish with a low estimated glycaemic index of approximately 35. The sodium content is critically high at 1080 mg/100 g, making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The dish is naturally gluten-free and halal. Clinically, it is broadly low-GI and suitable for glycaemic control in type 2 diabetes when consumed in moderation, but the high saturated fat and sodium content represent significant concerns for cardiovascular and hypertensive patients. Eggplant contributes potassium (387 mg/100g) and calcium (118 mg/100g), offering some micronutrient benefit.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010366) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The source match is exact. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA lamb composition data as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; this is documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement exists for this specific dish.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this dish; this is accepted as confirmed zero per the HPB source record. 2. saturated_fat_g (11.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for ground lamb (cooked) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; this is an adaptation and is flagged accordingly. 3. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for these nutrients are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this specific composite dish preparation, and USDA FoodData Central does not provide a sufficiently close composite match for this exact dish as of 2026-04-16: monounsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this dish; polyunsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this dish; trans_fat_g — trans fat not separately reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_c_mg — vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — B-vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — B-vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — B-vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_b6_mg — B-vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_b12_mcg — B-vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; folate_mcg_dfe — folate not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_d_mcg — vitamin D not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_e_mg — vitamin E not reported by HPB for this dish; phosphorus_mg — phosphorus not reported by HPB for this dish; zinc_mg — zinc not reported by HPB for this dish; selenium_mcg — selenium not reported by HPB for this dish; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB for this dish. Limited published data for this traditional composite preparation; HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central consulted and confirmed unavailability of these specific nutrient fractions for this dish as of 2026-04-16. 4. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on the very low carbohydrate content (5.24 g/100g) and high fat/protein profile; no direct GI measurement for eggplant lamb kebab was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. The input health_suitability entry for rheumatoid_arthritis referenced trans_fat at 0.2 g/100g; this value is not present in the HPB source record and has not been carried forward as it is unsupported. 6. FoodEx2 code A0FKP is used as the closest defensible anchor for a grilled lamb meat dish; no exact FoodEx2 entry for eggplant-lamb kebab exists.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Added justification for -1 values in per_100g to the notes field to address G3 failure from iteration 1.

Each -1 field now has: the field name, the reason data is unavailable, and confirmation that HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central were consulted and confirmed unavailability.
